resources.string: Something went wrong. Please try again.
Ongoing session timer while you train. Required during an active live workout.

While a live weight or cardio timer is running, ERV keeps a background timer so your session time stays accurate. Android requires a persistent notification for this—it is not used for ads or tracking. You can return from the notification (and, on supported devices, a bubble).

During an active live weight or cardio timer, ERV runs in the background so your session clock stays accurate. Android requires a persistent, ongoing notification for this—it is not optional while a timer is running, and it is not used for ads or tracking.

On (default): when you leave ERV during a live workout, Android may show a floating bubble (if your device allows it). Turn this off to use only the ongoing notification bar. You will still see the workout notification until you finish or cancel—that is required for the background timer. Use the button below if bubbles stay off because of system settings.

To allow bubbles: Settings → Apps → ERV → Notifications → open the “Live weight workout” or “Live cardio workout” category, then turn on Bubbles (wording varies by Android version). Start a live workout once so each category appears.
Floating bubbles are not available on this version of Android. An ongoing notification still appears during live workouts.
Notifications are off—live workout timer and return-to-workout actions may not work. Enable in system Settings.
Live workout in progress — tap to resume
Cardio timer running — tap to resume
Ongoing cardio session timer while you train. Required during an active cardio timer.
This is extra load on top of your body weight (for example a belt, vest, or plate). It does not include your full body weight.
Records your route during outdoor walk, run, hike, or ruck timer sessions when you allow GPS.
Recording GPS route — tap to return
Record GPS for outdoor walk, run, hike, ruck

When on, timer workouts for those outdoor activities can log distance from your route if you allow location access. Raw GPS point lists are never sent to Nostr relays (only session summaries sync). Android shows an ongoing notification while recording. Use the next option to control whether point lists are kept on this device after each workout is saved.
Keep detailed GPS track on device

When off, lat/lon point lists are not stored after a workout is saved. Distance, duration, estimated calories, and elevation gain/loss (when derived from the route) are still kept. Interactive maps, splits, exports, or other future features that need the full path may be limited.
Your raw GPS points are not included in encrypted cardio logs published to relays—those events only carry summaries. Full tracks exist on your phone only when this setting is on.
Workout logs (including GPS tracks when kept) live in app-private storage as JSON inside a DataStore file. Typical path:
You cannot open this file from Downloads. To back it up, use Android backup, adb, or your device manufacturer’s full backup tools—the app does not export this data for you.
%1$d GPS points · shape preview (not a street map)
Single GPS fix — longer outdoor sessions draw a full path
Elevation: GPS did not report enough altitude for this route (try again outdoors with a clear sky).

When GPS was recorded, uploads the route image and adds the link to your kind 1 note. NIP-96 uses one host; Blossom splits a public host (workout / social posts) from a private host (not used in notes—reserved for sensitive in-app media later).
Must expose /.well-known/nostr/nip96.json (example: https://nostr.build). Amber may need kind 27235 allowed.
Used for workout route images in kind 1 notes (same as typical kind 10063 entries). PUT …/upload; Amber may need kind 24242.
For personal images only. ERV does not upload these to Nostr or put their URLs in posts. Use a self-hosted or restricted server you trust.
Fetches your latest kind 10063 (Blossom server list) from the relays connected below—the same list other clients publish.
No kind 10063 found. Configure Blossom servers in another Nostr client with this same key, then try again.
Saved public Blossom server from your profile.
Sign in to load your Blossom server list.
No relay connection. Add relays below and wait until at least one shows connected.
Sign in and open Settings with relays connected to load your profile.

To show what is playing and use play, pause, and skip for your current audio app, enable notification access for “ERV media controls” in system settings. ERV does not read notification text for features—only the active media session. You can use volume below without this.
No active media session detected. Start playback in your music or podcast app, then return here.
